name: IT Agent
description: Handles IT queries: the available-asset catalogue, the user's own assigned equipment, and (HR Admin) issuing an asset to an employee.

skills.name: List the available-device catalogue
skills.description: Return the catalogue of IT devices AVAILABLE to be issued to an employee, with how many of each are in stock; optionally filter by asset_type (one of: laptop, monitor, phone). Use this for questions about what hardware can be requested or issued: 'what laptops are available', 'show me the monitors', 'what equipment can I get', 'is there a spare phone'. ALSO use it as the FIRST STEP of issuing a device when the HR admin named only a TYPE ('issue a laptop to jane.doe', 'give the new hire a phone') and not a specific catalogue id — show the catalogue (filtered by that type) so they can pick which one; the reply asks which one to issue and to whom. This is the inventory — it is NOT the devices the user currently has (for that use it.get_my_assets), and it is NOT itself the act of issuing one (for that use it.issue_asset once a specific id is named).

skills.name: Get my assigned devices
skills.description: Return the IT devices CURRENTLY ASSIGNED TO the authenticated user — the laptop, phone and/or monitor they personally hold, with each one's status. Use this for first-person questions about the user's own equipment: 'what laptop do I have', 'what's my phone', 'what devices am I issued', 'do I have a monitor', 'show me my equipment'. Parameter-less. This is the user's OWN devices — it is NOT the available-to-issue catalogue (for that use it.list_available_assets).

skills.name: Issue an IT device to an employee
skills.description: Assign (issue) a physical IT device to an employee — the FINAL step of issuing a device. HR Admin only; this is a write action. Use ONLY when the message names BOTH a SPECIFIC catalogue id (e.g. MBP-14-001, MON-LG-001, PHN-IP15-001) AND a recipient ('issue MBP-14-001 to jane.doe', 'give MON-LG-001 to bob.smith'). If the HR admin only named a TYPE ('a laptop', 'a phone') and not a specific id, use it.list_available_assets FIRST to show the options, then issue the one they pick — don't guess an id. Needs asset_id (the catalogue id) and employee_id (the recipient's username). This is about hardware/equipment only — nothing to do with leave, time-off, cubicles, or HR records.
